title: Bernardia dodecandra
intro: Bernardia dodecandra, also known as the bitter leaf or zobo plant, is a shrub species native to West Africa that has been traditionally used for its medicinal and culinary properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include the treatment of fever, malaria, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve immune function. The bioactive constituents of Bernardia dodecandra include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Bernardia dodecandra is often made into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed as a bitter tonic to stimulate digestion and boost overall health.
taxonomy: Bernardia dodecandra is classified within the family Salicaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the willow family. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics. The Salicaceae family is part of the larger order Malpighiales, which is a clade of flowering plants that also includes the spurge family and the euphorbia family. The order Malpighiales is then part of the larger clade eurosids I, which is a clade of flowering plants that also includes the rose family and the jasmine family. Bernardia dodecandra is a unique species within this classification, with its own distinct characteristics and evolutionary history.
